details.text: Derivative: قَدْ usually comes with the meaning of تَحْقيق (realisation) and تَوْكيد (affirmation) with the past tense to affirm that something has already happened.

Derivative: أَفْلَحَmeans to be successful. فَلَّاح in Arabic means a farmer. فَلَحَ and أَفْلَحَ originally relate to the act of splitting and tearing the soil to either plant the seed or to extract the crop when its ready. The meaning of success is derived from it because the tearing or splitting of the ground in the stages of farming crops represents success.

Derivative: الْمُؤْمِنُونَ “The believers” is in the active participle form (اِسْم الفاعِل) which is stronger than the verbal form like the statement الَّذِينَ آَمَنوا “Those who have believed” as the verbal form indicates a temporary state that is weak and liable to change whilst the active participle form (اِسْم الفاعِل) indicates to a permanency. It is therefore indicative of the “true” believers i.e., those who have established themselves as such.

Derivative: بَرْزَخٌ is a barrier and a big difference and also refers to the life of بَرْزَخٌ. بَرْزَخٌ literally means a barrier between two things. Here that is the life of this world and the life of the hereafter in the case where بَرْزَخٌ is the life in between which is very different.

Derivative: أَنسَابَ relations and ties. نَسَبَ means to describe and elaborate upon the relations you have with people. إِنْسَاب is to draw closeness in the relationship. Here أَنسَابَ refers to those close relationships that existed amongst people in the life of this world.

Derivative: كَالِح is the twisted and ugly face that somebody has when undergoing torture and where the lips cannot even touch each other due to the pain and anguish they are experiencing. كَالِح is in the فاعِل noun form instead of the verb form which indicates permanency i.e., that they will be in that state continuously.
Derivative: تَلْفَحُ to become distorted and discoloured by fire that in this case scorches the faces.
Derivative: شِقِوَة means شِقَاوَة which is referring to one’s mischievous, rebellious or bad side.
Derivative: ٱخْسَـُٔوا from خَسَأَ means to be shooed and driven away. It contains a meaning of contempt and humiliation in respect to the one whom it is being said to.

Derivative: الفِرْدَوْس is originally a Persian word and the English word Paradise also comes from it. Its meaning is paradise and is used for a luscious green valley with all kinds of fruits and flowers.

Derivative: الفَائِز is the one who is ultimately successful. It is from the words which falls under the category of لُغَةُ الاَضْدَاد (Language of opposites) in the case where فَوَّزَ الرَّجُل means that the man died. By connecting two meanings of success and death it can be deduced that the success is in something that if you did not succeed in it, you would die i.e., it is related to survival. If you succeed in it the rewards are huge but if you don’t succeed the consequences are extremely dire.
Derivative: العَادِّينَ are those who count and it is referring to the Angels who are keeping count and records.

Derivative: عَبَثًا means “without any purpose”. عَبَثَ refers to something that is important and serious in origin but is not taken seriously or with any importance or value attached to it whereby the person is only playing around.
Derivative: رَاحِم is the One who shows mercy immediately, currently and continuously i.e., here it is an acknowledgment of Allah’s mercy “even now” before the mercy on the Day of Judgement.

Derivative: سُلالَة comes from سَلَّ which means to unsheathe a sword and to pull out or extract the essence of something out of something. Here سُلالَة refers to the most useful extract (or essence) of the طِينٍ (clay or soil) that the human was created from.
Derivative: مَكِينٌ means stable and strong.
Derivative: قَرارٌ is a resting place.
Derivative: عَلَقَة is a hanging piece of flesh. Comes from عَلِقَ which means to hang (and cling).
Derivative: أَنْشَأَ - إِنْشاء means to create and also to raise.
Derivative: كَسَوْنا means we dressed and is from كَسَا – يَكْسُو which means to dress.
Derivative: مُضْغَة is that which looks like slightly chewed flesh and comes from مَضَغَ which means to chew i.e., a chunk or flesh that is undefined.
Derivative: طَرائِق refers to heavens or pathways.
- بِقَدَرٍ means by an exact calculation or measure.

Derivative: فَواكِه (fruits) the plural of فاكِهَة (fruit) comes from فَكِهَ which means to be happy and to smile in joy. And as such فَواكِه refers to fruits that taste so good that they make you happy and smile.

Derivative: خَاشِعُونَ is in the active participle form (اِسْم الفاعِل) and is indicative of permanence. It is referring to those who constantly have خُشوع in their prayer and the خُشوع is a constant feature of the prayer. The word is derived from خَشَعَ and in Arabic it expresses the meaning of having a fear or sense of awe that overwhelms the person until the muscles become numb and relaxed, similar to be paralysed by an overwhelming fear or sense of awe and feeling of being overpowered. The result is to be submissive and humbled.

Derivative: صِبْغ comes from صبْغَة which means colour or dye and صَبَغَ means to add colour to cloth or to dye it. Here it refers to the sauce or dip that comes from the oil of the tree and used in dishes.
Derivative: الْمَلَؤُا means chieftains or elders. It comes from the verb مَلَأَ which means to fill. They are called المَلأُ because they fill the court of the king.

Derivative: There is a difference in Arabic between غَرِيق and مُغْرَق. The غَرِيق is the one who drowns whilst the مُغْرَق is the one who is drowned. The Qawm of Nuh (as) didn’t just drown but were divinely drowned.
- وَفَارَ التَّنُّور means the pot boils over and is also used when one’s temper is lost and boils over. Here it refers to the water gushing out and over powerfully.
Derivative: To become even, balanced and level. Here in reference to the ship settling and landing.
Derivative: مُنْزَل is an اسْم المَفْعُول but it is also a ظَرْف and so مُنْزَل can indicate the time and place i.e., to the right or safe place to be set down and land.
Derivative: اللَّغْوُ refers to speech or an activity that does not contain any worldly or spiritual benefit i.e., it is idle and devoid of benefit.
Derivative: أَتْرَفَ means to provide and surround with luxury and the means to luxury.

Derivative: هَيْهَاتَ is an expression to indicate that something is far-fetched like: “How far-fetched!” It is used to mock somebody and to scold somebody to let them no that something is not going to happen, like: “No way!” It is to indicate and inform how far off something is from happening that somebody may hope for.

- Although Zakah is commonly used to refer to the obligatory Zakah which is given upon wealth (Zakat ul-Maal), here it is being used in the literal meaning of purification to indicate to the purification of the Nufoos (selves and personalities). This is supported by the use of فاعِلون which means that they do or undertake it and indicates to an active undertaking and constant working on oneself. This differs from the giving إيتاء or أَداء of the Zakah upon wealth.
- مَا is added to عَنْ for the purpose of Mubaalaghah (exaggeration).
Derivative: غُثاء is the foam of the sea that carries in it pebbles, straws and junk.
Derivative: بِالْحَقِّ means “in truth” and also “with purpose”.
Derivative: تَتْرَا means one after the other in succession.
Derivative: أَحَادِيثَ refers to news events or historical incidents that would be cited later on.
Derivative: عَالِينَ: عالٍ refers to somebody who thinks himself above other; arrogant and haughty.

Derivative: المُهْلَكِينَ from أَهْلَكَ are those who have been killed in a violent way or have been made to perish, in contrast to هَالِكِينَ from هَلَكَ those who have die or perish. المُهْلَكِينَ are those who have been given and dealt violent destruction.
Derivative: فُرُوج is the plural of فَرْج and refers to the private parts and includes the meaning of shame and temptations related to Zina and lewd immoral acts (Fawaahish).
Derivative: رَبْوَةٍ refers to a elevated patch of land with vegetation.
Derivative: آوَيْنَا from آَوَى to provide refuge from a danger.
Derivative: زُبُر literally pieces or chunks of iron and here it refers to divisions of groups or factions.
Derivative: غَمْرَة literally refers to the depths of the sea or a flood and here it implies the depths of unconsciousness and lack of awareness.
Derivative: مُشْفْقُونَ – To be frightened and afraid.

- مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَٰنُهُمْ – “What their right hands possessed” the word for possession is مَلَكَتْ which is the past tense (الماضِي) which indicates that this reality was one that already existed within the social structure of the society.
- The use of أَيْمان (right hands) carries significance because the right hand is an expression of agreements and the right hand is connected to oaths, where an oath in Arabic is named يَمين (i.e., the right hand). This informs us that whatever we possess is actually an agreement and a contract.
Derivative: وَجَل is to feel fear deep inside the heart. When the hair on the skin rises due to fear.
Derivative: يُسَارِعً from سَارَعَ to rush to win the race.
Derivative: سَابِق is the one who gets there first or wins the race.
Derivative: غَمْرَة refers to a deep state of unconsciousness.
Derivative: مُتْرَفُونَ refers to the financial elite and socialites i.e., those used to a luxurious life.
Derivative: يَجْأَرُ to cry out or scream in anguish for help like an animal.
- نَقَصَ عَلَى عَقِبَيْهِ means to turn away upon ones heals i.e., to turn away, retreat and withdraw. نَقَصَ means to turn away.

Derivative: مُسْتَكْبِرِينَ from اِسْتَكْبَرَ – اِسْتِكْبار and means to be arrogant. اِسْتِكْبار differs from تَكَبُّر in the case where تَكَبُّر refers to those who actually have power and show off or act arrogantly whilst اِسْتِكْبار refers to the one who wants to have power and want to act as though they are better and higher.

Derivative: تَهْجُرُونَ to migrate from, abandon and leave. Here, it means to leave and abandon that which the tongue is meant to be used for by discussing and saying things that should not be said in terms of inappropriate speech.
Derivative: سَامِر refers to evening or nighttime entertainment and storytelling. Here it refers to a group sitting around the Ka’bah at night and speaking with each other.
Derivative: اِبْتَغى – اِبْتِغاء – to pursue or seek. بَغي is used to refer to going outside of or exceeding bounds. اِبْتَغى is a strong pursuit.
Derivative: ٱلْعَادُونَ means to cross the line and exceed the limits. It comes from عَدا which means to be aggressive and cross the line i.e., the boundaries of the Halal and Haram.
- “Their Dhikr (mention)” meaning that which relates to them in the Qur’an i.e., it is talking about them and their reality and yet still turn away in opposition to it.

- Here both خَرْجًا and خَرَاجُ are used and both are the Masdar for خَرَجَ. When more wordings are used it is indicative of their being more meanings. خَرْج is a an amount of money whilst خَرَاج is a larger amount of money. When they give financial aid or make a payment خَرْج is used but when Allah is the giver خَرَاج is used indicating that what He provides is better. This is affirmed further by the statement: وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ “And He (Allah) is the best of Providers”.
Derivative: نَاكِبُونَ from نَكَبَ which means to turn or swerve away.
Derivative: طُغْيان means in excess of rebellion i.e., tyranny.
Derivative: يَعْمَهُونَ from عَمِهَ to wander about and stray aimlessly and blindly. عَمِيَ is used for the blindness of the eyes whilst عَمِهَ is used for the blindness of the hearts.
Derivative: أَفْئِدَة the plural of فُؤَاد means the strong or overwhelmed and emotional heart. فُؤَاد can also mean a sound intellect and strong mind.
Derivative: ذَرَأَ means to spread out, create and allow to sprout (throughout the land). It is used to describe the way the farmer spreads seeds in the ground.
Derivative: أَمَانَة comes from أَمَن which means peace. It means that someone is at peace entrusting you with something. It is a trust that has been given to you.
Derivative: رَاعُون is the plural active participle meaning to shepherd and watch over. راعٍ means a shepherd.
Derivative: عَهْد is a promise that has been made where you take responsibility for fulfilling it.

Derivative: أَساطِير comes from أُسْطُورَة which means a legend, folk tale or fable. It is originally a Greek word that was adopted into Arabic and the English word “story” also has its origins in it.
Derivative: تَذَكَّرُونَ means to make effort to remember or to allow yourself to be reminded, to take advice and heed.
Derivative: تَتَّقُونَ means to take precautions to protect yourselves.
Derivative: يُجِيرُ to provide refuge comes from the word جَارٌ which means neighbour. يُجِيرُ implies to give refuge to a neighbour.
Derivative: تُسْحَرُونَ to be mystified, bewitched or enchanted.
Derivative: هَمَزَٰتِ means the poking and prodding.
